back_translation: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"Command the sons of Israel, and they shall send away from the camp everyone with a skin disease, and everyone with a discharge, and everyone defiled by a corpse.
From male to female you shall send them away — send them outside the camp, so they will not defile their camps, in which I dwell among them."
And the sons of Israel did so, and sent them outside the camp; as the LORD spoke to Moses, so the sons of Israel did.
And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"Speak to the sons of Israel: a man or woman, when they do any of the sins of mankind, acting unfaithfully against the LORD — and that soul is guilty —
they shall confess their sin which they have done, and he shall restore the full value of his guilt-payment, and add a fifth to it, and give it to the one against whom he has done wrong.

But if the man has no kinsman-redeemer to whom the guilt-payment can be restored, the guilt-payment that is restored belongs to the LORD, to the priest, besides the ram of atonement by which atonement is made for him.
And every contribution from all the holy gifts of the sons of Israel which they bring to the priest shall belong to him.
And each man's holy gifts shall be his own; whatever a man gives to the priest shall belong to him."
And the LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
"Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: any man whose wife goes astray and acts unfaithfully against him —

and a man lies with her with intercourse, and it is hidden from her husband's eyes, and she has concealed it, and she has defiled herself; and there is no witness against her, and she was not caught —
and a spirit of jealousy comes over him, and he is jealous of his wife, and she is defiled — or a spirit of jealousy comes over him, and he is jealous of his wife, but she is not defiled —

then the man shall bring his wife to the priest, and shall bring her offering on her behalf, a tenth of an ephah of barley flour; he shall not pour oil over it, and he shall not put frankincense on it, for it is a grain offering of jealousy, a grain offering of memorial, a reminder of iniquity.
And the priest shall present her and have her stand before the LORD.
And the priest shall take holy water in a clay vessel; and from the dust which is on the floor of the tabernacle the priest shall take, and put it into the water.

And the priest shall have the woman stand before the LORD, and shall loosen the head (hair) of the woman, and shall put on her palms the grain offering of memorial — it is the grain offering of jealousy; and in the hand of the priest shall be the bitter waters that bring a curse.

And the priest shall put her under oath and say to the woman, 'If no man has lain with you and you have not gone astray to defilement under your husband, be free from these bitter waters that bring a curse.
But you — when you have gone astray under your husband, and when you have defiled yourself, and a man has given his sleeping (intercourse) into you besides your husband' —

then the priest shall put the woman under the oath of cursing, and the priest shall say to the woman, 'May the LORD make you a curse and an oath among your people, in the LORD's making your thigh fall and your belly swell.
And these waters that bring a curse shall enter into your bowels to cause your belly to swell and your thigh to fall.' And the woman shall say, 'Amen, Amen.'
And the priest shall write these curses in a scroll, and shall blot them out into the waters of bitterness.
And he shall make the woman drink the bitter waters that bring a curse, and the waters that bring a curse shall enter her for bitterness.
And the priest shall take from the woman's hand the grain offering of jealousy, and shall wave the grain offering before the LORD, and shall bring it to the altar.
And the priest shall take a handful from the grain offering, namely its memorial portion, and shall burn it on the altar; and after that he shall make the woman drink the water.

And he shall make her drink the water, and it shall happen — if she is defiled and has acted unfaithfully against her husband — that the waters that bring a curse shall enter into her for bitterness, and her belly shall swell and her thigh shall fall, and the woman shall become a curse in the midst of her people.
But if the woman is not defiled and she is clean, then she shall be free and shall conceive seed (children).
This is the law of jealousy when a wife goes astray under her husband and defiles herself,
or a man over whom a spirit of jealousy passes, and he is jealous of his wife; he shall have the woman stand before the LORD, and the priest shall do to her all this law.
And the man shall be free from iniquity, but that woman shall bear her iniquity."
